sourceMovie.title: Cannibal Holocaust
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cannibal Holocaust works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Friday the 13th
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cannibal Holocaust clicked for you, Friday the 13th (1980) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cannibal Holocaust?
faq.answer: Try Friday the 13th, Wrong Turn, The Last House on the Left—each shares DNA with Cannibal Holocaust while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cannibal Holocaust?
faq.answer: Cannibal Holocaust (1980) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cannibal Holocaust worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
